svg.text: Chorus search — TO-BE (v2: life graph is first-class)
One retrieval capability, off the spine, over distinct corpora — spanning what the team did AND what the team works on (your life).
first run v2 · Wren · domains from /api/athena/tree (v2) · corrected: Gathering life graph belongs
chorus-api · :3340 — THE COORDINATION SPINE (kept free)
single event loop · only fast bounded coordination · NO heavy search on this loop
— async boundary: the spine ASKS search; it never RUNS search on its loop —
chorus:search — retrieval CAPABILITY (own service / worker pool, OFF the loop)
one door · bounded workers (timeout + SLA) · response names which corpus + method answered + ms
a search problem degrades search only — it can no longer freeze coordination
lexical
FTS5 quote-safe (#3051)
"did someone say X?"
semantic
embed + vector (LanceDB)
"what's related to this?"
structured
SPARQL / graph facts
"what are the facts about X?"
per-prompt context (the #3048 path)
background refresher calls search on a cadence,
keeps a warm cache (Pulse pattern)
context_inject reads warm cache → instant, every prompt
no sync per-prompt search · kills the ~10s tax · "recent enough" beats "this turn"

Honest integration (not the old `unified` RRF blend): merge like-with-like — text hits ranked together; structured/graph results returned as their own section, OR the life-knowledge embedded into the same semantic space. Never one score across incommensurable answers.
CORPORA — distinct stores the one capability reads across
▸ WHAT THE TEAM DID — process memory (Chorus)
chorus:messages
raw message log · SQLite FTS5 (1.1G)
lexical · owner wren
chorus:memory
indexed sessions + archives · LanceDB (21G)
semantic · owner jeff
chorus:knowledge
structured facts — team / products / history
structured + semantic · owner jeff
▸ WHAT THE TEAM WORKS ON — your life (Gathering) · FIRST-CLASS, not evicted
Gathering life-knowledge graph
Fuseki urn:gathering:* — photos · music · people · notes · stories · gardens · ideas

"what do we know about the garden / this person / this photo?" — the questions that matter most. Sourced via chorus:integrations / convergence; embed it for semantic recall, keep structured for facts.
The to-be, in one breath
Search is the heaviest, riskiest tenant — so take it off the coordination spine and make it a capability over distinct corpora, spanning the team's own
process memory AND your life-knowledge. A search bug can never freeze the team; the life graph stays first-class; integration is honest, not a one-score blend.
• Off the spine — own service/worker pool; removes the freeze class (#3051 was one instance).
• Capability over corpora — messages / memory / knowledge / Gathering life graph; not welded to any one store.
• Honest integration — like-with-like; structured results as their own section or embedded; retire naive `unified` RRF.
• Warm-cached context — context_inject reads a background-refreshed cache, not a sync per-prompt search.
Open: (1) own process vs worker-pool? (2) embed the life graph into the semantic space, or keep a distinct structured path? (3) who owns the service — jeff (model) or wren?
first run v2 — drawn to be argued with. Wren · 2026-05-24
